title: [FIRRTL] Lower Types Regression
body:
The following circuit is throwing a verification error after lower types:

```scala
circuit Foo :
  extmodule Bar :
    input a: UInt<2>

  module Foo :
    wire a : { flip b : UInt<1>}
    inst bar of Bar
    bar.a is invalid
    a.b <= bar.a
```

This is resulting in a `tail` of a flip type. 😬 The error is:
```
Foo.fir:9:9: error: input must be integer and amount must be >= 0
    a.b <= bar.a
```

The problematic line that is causing this is:
```mlir
 %0 = "firrtl.tail"(%bar_a) {amount = 1 : i32} : (!firrtl.flip<uint<2>>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
```
